direct equivalency to a UMD course (whether within or outside the major),

•

For detailed instructions on requesting a transfer course evaluation for domestic transfer credit, international transfer credit, Education Abroad transfer credit, or for obtaining a Preliminary Transfer Credit Evaluation, see registrar.umd.edu/transfer- credit/transfer-course-evaluation-process.

no direct equivalency, but satisfies general education requirement, or

•

All departments reserve the right to reevaluate the content of exams/experience, and to change the minimum required score, course equivalencies, and the assignment of credit.

• no direct equivalency, but is accepted as a general elective.

Students work with an academic advisor to understand how transfer courses satisfy degree requirements:

Students are responsible for submitting all final official transcripts detailing their entire academic record prior to enrolling at UMD. Transfer courses will post to a student’s UMD record only from official transcripts received from the institution at which the credit was earned.

Students will not receive credit for both PLC and completing an equivalent course. No credit will be awarded for AP, IB, or A-Level/AS-Level exams that are repeated or taken after a student has matriculated to the University of Maryland.

Students who have been admitted to UMD should contact their advising college for assistance.
